channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Celtic make first new summer signing as Duran joins from Qarabag
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Celtic have made striker Camilo Duran their first new summer signing in a deal worth around £6m.

item.title: World Cup: Belgium can shock Spain despite underdog tag — Garcia
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Belgium head coach Rudi Garcia says his players are unfazed by predictions that Spain will eliminate them in Friday's 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-final, insisting the Red Devils have the quality to pull off one of the tournament's biggest upsets. The post World Cup: Belgium can shock Spain despite u

item.title: Super Eagles Winger Renews Contract With Bulgarian Club Botev Plodiv
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigerian winger Samuel Kalu has signed a new contract with Bulgarian club Botev Plovdiv, committing his future to the Yellow and Blacks until 2029. Kalu had been heavily linked with a move away from the club, with fellow Bulgarian outfit Levski Sofia and several Swiss clubs reportedly monitoring his

item.title: Archbishop of Benin Diocese Augustine Akubeze dies at 69
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Chancellor of the Catholic Archdiocese of Benin City, Very Rev. Fr. Michael Oyanoafoh, announced the passing of the cleric in a statement on Thursday.

item.title: Okpehbolo, Obaseki Mourn Passing Of Archbishop Akubueze
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Edo State Governor, Senator Monday Okpebholo, and former governor of the State, Godwin Obaseki have mourned the passing of the Archbishop of the Catholic Archdiocese of Benin City, Most Rev. Augustine Obiora Akubeze. In a statement issued by the Chief Press Secretary to Governor Okpebholo, Dr Patric

item.title: Nigeria becomes Africa’s highest-ranked country for Responsible AI
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Nigeria's ranking comes as governments globally race to establish governance frameworks that keep pace with rapid advances in artificial intelligence.

item.title: Ziemba: Alternates Pipelines as Hormuz Pressure Remains
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Risks around the Strait of Hormuz remain elevated as U.S.-Iran tensions pressure a fragile truce. Ziemba Insights founder Rachel Ziemba tells Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa that she sees more commitment to alternative pipeline routes ahead. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Fubara to present N1.84tn 2026 budget to Rivers Assembly
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Rivers State Governor, Siminalayi Fubara, is billed to present the 2026 Appropriation Bill before the State House of Assembly on Friday, in what would mark a significant step toward ending months of fiscal uncertainty in the state. The presentation follows the Assembly’s approval, two weeks ago

item.title: Taraba 2027: PDP guber candidate raises alarm over govt use of EFCC to intimidate opposition
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Following Wednesday's invitation and subsequent detention of Senator Emmanuel Bwacha, the governorship candidate of the People's Democratic Party PDP in Taraba State for the 2027 general elections, his campaign organisation has accused the State Governor Agbu Kefas of using State institutions to int

item.title: South Sudan: Fifteen Years of Independence for South Sudan, but Still Little to Celebrate
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [RFI] South Sudan, the world's youngest country, on Thursday marks the 15th anniversary of its independence, following decades of war with Sudan, a 2005 peace agreement and a 2011 self-determination referendum in which almost 99 percent of voters backed statehood. But the hopes that came with it hav

item.title: Canal+ completes MultiChoice acquisition
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The French media giant says the deal will strengthen MultiChoice's operations across Africa and expand its international reach. The post Canal+ completes MultiChoice acquisition appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Adamawa Govt Reacts To ADC Guber Candidate’s N3m Fine
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …says it’s not about politics rather responsibility. The Adamawa State Government has reiterated its commitment to enforcing the law against the illegal pasting of political posters on public infrastructure across the state. The State government said it’s position aimed at protecti

item.title: Bodies found in cars as deadly Spain wildfire kills at least 12
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Bodies found in cars as deadly Spain wildfire kills at least 12

item.title: Enugu State Forest Guard issues standing operational order
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Enugu State Forest Guard has issued a new Order introducing comprehensive standards governing the dress code, operational turnout, accoutrements, corporate identity, discipline, and professional appearance of all officers and operatives of the Corps. The post Enugu State Forest Guard issues stan

item.title: State police: Mbah presses for financial autonomy, investment in technology
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governor Peter Mbah of Enugu State has called for financial autonomy and investment in modern security infrastructure for state police services when created, stressing the need for any legal framework for state police to take into account the need for national standards, national support, local capa

item.title: Police Arrest Driver Who Stole Employer’s Car in Ondo
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has arrested a suspected vehicle thief in Abuja and recovered a Hyundai saloon car allegedly stolen from his employer in Akure. The post Police Arrest Driver Who Stole Employer’s Car in Ondo appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Xenophobia: FG repatriates 282 more Nigerians from South Africa
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has evacuated another 282 Nigerians from South Africa as part of its ongoing repatriation exercise following the wave of xenophobic attacks targeting foreign nationals in the country. The post Xenophobia: FG repatriates 282 more Nigerians from South Africa appeared first on Th

item.title: Administration without sovereignty will not free Palestine
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The end of Hamas governance in Gaza may change who administers it, but not who holds power over Palestinian life.

item.title: Solar Now Supplies 20% Of Nigeria’s Electricity, May Hit 50% By 2028 – REA
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Solar power generation has risen dramatically in the last few years to about 20 per cent of Nigeria’s electricity supply and could reach 50 per cent by 2029 if current deployment and private-sector partnerships continue, the Rural Electrification Agency (REA) said. REA managing director, Abba Abubak

item.title: Nigerian Stock Market Records 68% Year-to-date Returns, Emerges World’s Top-performing
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian equities have overtaken South Korea’s Kospi index to deliver the highest dollar-based returns of any market in the world this year, as souring sentiment toward artificial intelligence stocks pushes the Asian nation’s once world-beating rally into bear territory. The benchmark in

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) has arraigned The post ICPC arraigns FRSC officer over alleged N4.31m job racketeering appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Police rescue abducted couple unhurt after gun battle with kidnappers in Oyo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has rescued an abducted couple unhurt following a coordinated security operation in the Oke-Ogun area of the state. The post Police rescue abducted couple unhurt after gun battle with kidnappers in Oyo appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: ‎Acknowledging the fierce and competitive nature of the party’s internal democratic processes, Akanmidu noted that every aspirant and supporter acted out of a genuine passion to serve Kogi West, but maintained that the party has spoken through its constitutional mechanisms and its decisions deserve

item.title: The Korean Levered ETFs Shaking Markets All Around the World
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Barclays's Alexander Altmann explains the exploding demand.

item.title: Titilade Ilesanmi: Mums, Shopping is Not How to Rebuild Your Style Confidence
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: There was a time when I felt disconnected from my style. Not because I didn’t love fashion. I’ve always loved fashion, but somewhere between becoming a mum, navigating body changes, managing responsibilities, and simply doing life, I no longer felt like the woman I used to be. My body had changed. M

item.title: Odd Lots: Korea’s Levered ETFs Shake Up Global Markets (Podcast)
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Retail participation in the stock market is booming. And of course the biggest story in markets is the AI trade, which has created an incredible amount of demand for chips and memory. These two broad themes have come together in the form of leveraged, single-stock ETFs. And while these products are

item.title: Bayeux Tapestry smuggled into Britain for first visit in 1,000 years
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The loan of the artwork is seen as symbolic of French-British relations as London works to mend ties post-Brexit.

item.title: Love not same after year 2020 – Tekno claims
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian singer, Augustine Kelechi, popularly known as Tekno, has shared his observation about love over the years, claiming that since after 2020, love has not been the same. Speaking in a recent interview with Cool FM Lagos, the ‘Pana’ crooner said he believes people grew fatigue of lo

item.title: NIA Urges Flood Victims To Notify Insurers Promptly
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian Insurers Association (NIA) has urged policyholders affected by recent flooding in parts of Lagos State to promptly notify their insurers and begin the claims process. Chairperson of the association, Ebelechukwu Nwachukwu, made the appeal in a statement on Thursday in Lagos. Nwachukwu, who i

item.title: Maritime Trade Faces Disruption After Fresh US-Iran Strikes
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Fresh military exchanges between the United States and Iran have triggered another wave of disruption to global maritime trade, with shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz falling sharply following attacks on three commercial tankers earlier this week. Data from maritime intelligence firm Kpl

item.title: FG Signs 20-Year Gas Supply Deal To Revive Ajaokuta Steel Plant
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has taken a major step towards reviving the long-idle Ajaokuta Steel Company, with the Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) Limited and Ajaokuta Steel Company Limited signing a 20-year gas supply agreement to support the plant's resuscitation. item.description: Israel has shared intelligence with the United States alleging that Iran recently developed a new plot to assassinate President Donald Trump, according to multiple US media reports. item.title: Lagos court remands driver accused of stabbing couple during drinks bill dispute
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A 40-year-old commercial bus driver, Kabiru Olajide, has been remanded at the Ikoyi Correctional Centre after appearing before an Igbosere Magistrate’s Court in Lagos over allegations that he stabbed a husband and wife during a dispute over an unpaid N75,000 drinks bill. Olajide was arraigned

item.title: EFCC arraigns ex-bank employee over alleged N294.6m theft in Lagos
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Lagos Zonal Directorate 2 of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission, EFCC, has arraigned a former employee of a new-generation bank, Chinonso Akujobi, before Justice I.O. Ijelu of the Lagos State High Court sitting in Ikeja over an alleged theft of N294,595,992. Akujobi was brought before the

item.title: Troops Arrest Chadian Woman Supplying Drugs To Terrorists In Borno
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Troops of Operation Hadin Kai have arrested a 48-year-old Chadian woman suspected of transporting illicit drugs and other prohibited items allegedly meant for terrorist groups operating in the Lake Chad region. The suspect was apprehended during an intelligence-led operation by troops of Sector 3 al

item.title: Divorce frees you — Media personality Hauwa claims
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Hauwa claimed that divorce frees unhappy people from the ties of marriage, allowing them to find happiness again.

item.description: The Africa Democratic Congress, ADC, has vowed to hold President Bola Tinubu personally accountable if anything happens to former Kaduna State governor, Nasir El-Rufai while he is in custody of the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission, ICPC. item.title: PDP chieftain backs Adebutu’s 2027 Ogun governorship bid
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The National Woman Leader of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), Prof. Adenike Ogunshe, has expressed confidence that the party’s governorship candidate in Ogun State, Oladipupo Adebutu, will win the 2027 governorship election. Ogunshe stated this on Thursday during a thank-you visit to Adebut

item.title: Zazzau Emir calls for electronic voting in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Emir of Zazzau, Ambassador Ahmad Nuhu Bamalli, has urged Nigeria to adopt electronic voting machines for all elections, saying technology will make the electoral process more transparent, credible and efficient. He made the call on Thursday in Kaduna during the launch of the Kaduna State Indepen

item.title: ‘You are clueless’ – Presidency hits Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu’s Special Adviser on Information and Strategy, Bayo Onanuga, has described the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate, Peter Obi as clueless. Onanuga was reacting to an interview where Obi recalled how he called on former Presidents Olusegun Obasanjo and

item.title: Japan’s Top Pension Fund Likely to Brush Off Political Pressure
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Japan’s biggest public pension fund will likely ignore Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama’s call to boost domestic investment, at least in the short run, because of strict rules governing asset allocation and its public mandate.

item.description: Spain and Belgium will lock horns in one of the biggest matches of the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarter-finals today, Friday. The post World Cup: Everything to know about Spain vs Belgium quarter-final match appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.description: "They want to take out the US leader — me. I'm on whatever list. I saw this morning I'm on every single one of their lists," Trump told reporters aboard Air Force One as he flew home from a NATO summit. item.title: Nigeria Ranks Africa’s First On Global AI Ranking Index
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria has emerged as the leading African country in the second edition of the Global Index on Responsible Artificial Intelligence AI (GIRAI), ranking 38th globally and 1st in Africa with an overall index score of 45.93. This was disclosed in a statement issued on Thursday by Isime Esene, media aid

item.title: People called me lazy after my career declined – Tekno
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Music star, Augustine Kelechi, alias Tekno, has opened up about the stigmatisation he faced while battling severe vocal cord damage from acid reflux that led to his career decline. DAILY POST recalls that Tekno, at the peak of his fame in 2018-2021, suffered severe vocal cord damage, during which he

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Marvel has already confirmed plans for an X-Men reboot, directed by Jake Schreier, which is currently scheduled for release in 2028.

item.description: The Group managing director of Dangote Cement Plc, Arvind Pathak, has declared that Africa is well-positioned to lead the next phase of sustainable industrial growth by accelerating decarbonization while expanding cement production to meet the continent’s rising infrastructure needs. item.title: Nigerian Stocks Overtake South Korea to Deliver World’s Highest Dollar Returns
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nigeria’s stock market has emerged as the world’s top performer in dollar terms this year, overtaking South Korea after a sharp sell-off in Asian equities weakened the Kospi’s record rally. According to data from 92 global stock exchanges, Nigerian equities have returned 68 per cen

item.description: The Social Democratic Party (SDP) on Thursday expressed commitment to party unity, internal reforms, and preparations for the 2027 general election after its second National Executive Committee (NEC) meeting in Abuja. item.title: Iran had 'specific' plan to kill Trump, US media report citing Israeli intel
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Iran planned to kill US President Donald Trump in a fresh plot unveiled by Israel, several US media outlets report. According to Israeli intelligence, the plan was “specific” and may explain why Trump chose to switch planes when leaving the NATO summit in Turkey earlier this week.

item.title: Ethiopia: Could Abiy’s term-limit plan reset the clock, keep him in power longer?
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed says a two-term limit would strengthen Ethiopia’s democracy. But attention has quickly shifted to whether the National Dialogue is preparing the country for a presidential republic that could give him a fresh political mandate.

item.title: Ikate two-bedroom apartment prices hit N205 million as off-plan demand grows
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The average selling price of two-bedroom apartments in Ikate rose to N205 million in 2026, more than doubling over five years as strong off-plan demand gathered pace. item.title: Firm upgrades solar system for Abuja mall
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Grene Capital, an assets manager has said it has completed a major solar expansion at Jabi Lake Mall in Abuja, further strengthening the asset’s position as one of Nigeria’s leading retail and lifestyle destinations. The mall’s original 600 kWp solar installation established Jabi L

item.title: Oyebamiji Felicitates Akinola, Omowaiye, Badmus On Birthday
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Governorship Candidate of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in the forthcoming gubernatorial election in Osun State, Asiwaju Munirudeen Bola Oyebamiji (AMBO) has hailed President Bola Tinubu’s appointees, Dr Charles Akinola, Engr. Oluremi Omowaiye and Engr. Olalekan Abdul-Rahman Badmus o

item.title: United Nigeria supports young visually impaired singers
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: In its bid to promote inclusivity, United Nigeria Airlines (UNA) has supported young talents who took part in a singing competition organised for people living with disabilities. UNA co-sponsored the blind segment of the ‘Henrotion in Expression’ event held in Port Harcourt recently, whi

item.title: ‘Unstoppable force’: Mbappe’s France crush Morocco’s World Cup dreams
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Morocco came to the quarterfinal with high hopes and revenge on their minds, only to be met with the in-form team.

item.title: ANAN holds MCPD, recommends strengthening revenue systems
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Association of National Accountants of Nigeria (ANAN’s) 3rd session of Mandatory Continuing Professional Development, MCPD programme has opened in Kano with a call on professional accountants to continue to strengthen governance, protect public resources and contribute to national development. T

item.title: Vanguard Buys Inflation-Protected Bonds on an Oil-Market Oddity
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Unusual moves in an oil-market gauge are prompting Vanguard Asset Management Ltd. to buy insurance against stickier-than-expected US inflation.

item.title: Actor Frederick Leonard Subtly Reacts To Viral Divorce Reports
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Nollywood actor Frederick Leonard shared a short message on his Instagram page amid ongoing reports of his divorce from fellow actor Peggy Ovire..The actor wrote: "It's Always Love and Gratitude from here."

item.title: Police arrest 21 suspects over alleged cybercrime, fraud cases
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Kingsley Omonobi ABUJA — The Nigeria Police Force says it has arrested 21 suspects in connection with separate cybercrime investigations, including an alleged fraud involving the manipulation of MTN Nigeria’s billing system. Force Public Relations Officer, CSP Anietie Okonko Iniedu, disclos

item.title: Audit executives warn over internal audit marginalisation
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Audit executives have cautioned against internal audit marginalisation, saying it can hurt business organisations. According to Festus Ogunmokun, former Chief Audit Executive, Nigerian Institute of Management and West African Portland Cement Company (WAPCO), the consequences of marginalising interna

item.title: Dwayne Johnson set to become X-Men’s next major villain in Marvel shocker
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Hollywood star Dwayne Johnson could be making a major move into the Marvel Cinematic Universe, with reports linking him to the role of iconic X-Men villain Apocalypse.

item.title: Flood claims life of commercial motorcyclist in Delta
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Egufe Yafugborhi & Ochuko Akuopha, Asaba ASABA — A commercial motorcyclist has died after he was swept away by floodwaters in Agbor, headquarters of Ika South Local Government Area of Delta State. The incident occurred on Wednesday night around Imudia Street following a torrential rainfall th

item.title: Catholic Archbishop Of Benin, Akubeze, Dies Weeks Before Retirement
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Catholic Archdiocese of Benin City has been thrown into mourning following the death of its Archbishop, Most Rev. Augustine Obiora Akubeze, barely a month before his retirement. Archbishop Akubeze reportedly died peacefully in his sleep while on vacation outside Nigeria on Thursday, July 9. He w

item.title: Group seeks support to eradicate women’s poverty
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Women For Women International Nigeria has sought intervention for improved living conditions of the female gender, mobilising stakeholders to ensure a better society for them. The group’s country director Bukola Onyishi stated this during the group’s 25th anniversary, expressing concerns
